description_long: This object is a traditional water well featuring a circular stone wall constructed from dark, weathered bricks, set atop a square cobblestone platform. The well is topped with a wooden gabled roof supported by two sturdy wooden posts, providing shelter from above. The stonework appears aged and slightly irregular, suggesting hand-laid masonry, while the wooden roof has a rough, natural grain texture. The interior of the well is open and unsealed, allowing access to the water below. There are no visible mechanisms for drawing water, such as a pulley or bucket. The overall design is utilitarian, with no decorative embellishments, and the structure is robust and intended for outdoor urban or park settings.
description: A circular stone water well with a wooden gabled roof, set on a square cobblestone base. The well is open-topped and lacks any visible water-drawing mechanism or decorative features.
description_view_0: Front view showing the stone well, wooden posts, and the flat underside of the wooden roof, with the cobblestone base clearly visible.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the triangular gabled roof and the open well mouth, with the wooden support structure in full view.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, displaying the stone wall, wooden posts, and the roof's underside, with the cobblestone base prominent.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the gabled roof's peak and the open well, with the wooden support structure and cobblestone base visible.
attribute_well_cover_design: A-frame wooden gabled roof, open underneath, no lid or slab covering the well opening.
attribute_surrounding_protective_structu: Circular stone wall approximately waist-high, serving as a protective barrier around the well opening.
